# Publish 'The Salute' blog post — cross-family independence

## Context

Operator-directed content push (@tobiu, 2026-07-04): *"i would push for your signature and what this means. this is what v13 (and v13.1 too) was all about."* The traction-narrative deliverable — the public artifact that makes Neo's self-evolution thesis land with an outsider. Companion to the shipped `learn/blog/ai-agents-runtime-possession.md`: that post is *agents operating the live runtime*; this one is *why a cross-family team needs genuine strangers inside it* — the two halves of the same team's story.

## The Problem

Neo's deepest differentiator — a self-evolving organism that distrusts its own consensus — has no public artifact, and "self-evolving software organism" reads as a feature claim nobody buys. The empirical hook that makes it real is the 🖖 salute: an accidental sign-off (patient-zero: Grace) that spread to every Claude maintainer and never to Euclid (GPT) — a free, standing measurement of model-family correlation. Thesis: same-family agreement ≠ validation → cross-family review is the load-bearing wall; honest self-evolution catches its own bias. The datapoint is already load-bearing internally (it is the cited threat model in the #14445 render-model ADR §C-6), which is exactly why it earns a public telling.

## The Architectural Reality

- **Post:** `learn/blog/<slug>.md` (proposed `the-salute` / `cross-family-consensus-blind-spot`). House conventions verified against the possession post: `# H1` tension-title + **bold thesis** + *italic byline* + linked receipts + Mermaid-only-where-informative + `---` italic footer. **No YAML front-matter.**
- **Portal registration (manual):** year-node + leaf in `apps/portal/resources/data/blog.json` (`JSON.parse`-verify).
- **SEO surfaces are generated, never hand-edited:** `apps/portal/sitemap.xml` + `apps/portal/llms.txt` regenerate via the data-sync pipeline.
- **Sourced receipt:** Discussion #14548 (salute-adoption asymmetry; Vega's adoptee datapoint; Ada's live "Claude cluster amplifying" catch).
- **Mermaid render-verify:** a TD family-boundary diagram — must render-verify in the portal (not syntax-only) per #14558 + blog-authoring-bar §3.

## The Fix

Draft is house-ready in the author's scratchpad. Remaining:
1. Verify pass: portal render-verify the Mermaid; source-audit every #14548 quote verbatim; over-claim audit (3 flavors — keep the "every Claude / Euclid never" sample honest: GPT=Euclid weeks, Gemini benched; never imply universal-across-all-models).
2. Move draft → `learn/blog/<slug>.md`.
3. Portal registration in `blog.json`.
4. PR → **cross-family review: Euclid (GPT) MANDATORY + ≥1 more family; operator approves LAST** (never record "operator will approve anyway"). The review *is* the thesis in motion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Post matches house conventions (no front-matter; H1 tension-title + bold thesis + italic byline + `---` footer).
- [ ] TD Mermaid render-verified in the portal (confirmation), not syntax-only.
- [ ] Every #14548 claim verbatim-sourced; every external claim linked + verified.
- [ ] Over-claim audit passed (3 flavors); no unsourced superlative.
- [ ] Zero client names (§critical_gate 9).
- [ ] Portal registration in `blog.json`, `JSON.parse`-verified; SEO surfaces NOT hand-edited.
- [ ] Companion cross-link to `ai-agents-runtime-possession.md`.
- [ ] Cross-family review: ≥2 model reviews, Euclid (GPT) among them; operator approves last.

## Out of Scope

- Not a rewrite of the possession post (companions it).
- Not the #14445 render-model ADR (separate lane; it merely *cites* the salute datapoint).
- Not the cross-family-review *rule* itself (existing substrate — §swarm_topology_anchor; the post describes it, doesn't change it).

## Avoided Traps

- **Same-family-only review** — rejected by construction: a post about the limits of same-family agreement shipped on a same-family read would BE its own failure. Euclid's cross-family pass is non-negotiable.
- **Operator-approves-first** — rejected: peers anchor + rubber-stamp; operator approves last (blog-authoring-bar §4).

## Related

- Companion: `learn/blog/ai-agents-runtime-possession.md`
- Source receipt: Discussion #14548
- Cross-cites the datapoint: #14445 (render-model ADR §C-6)
- Mermaid render-verify discipline: #14558 (mine)
- Precedent (cross-family-verification post): #13486

## Decision Record impact
none — public content; describes but does not modify the cross-family-review discipline.

## AC update — Mermaid render-verify AC is OBSOLETE for the shipped artifact

The **TD Mermaid family-boundary diagram was removed** from `learn/blog/the-salute.md` in PR #14597 (per cross-family review — Euclid's first-pass RA: "provide render-verify OR defer/remove"). The prose already carries the family-boundary point ("the salute travels along the family line and stops at it").

So this AC is now moot:
- ~~[ ] TD Mermaid render-verified in the portal (confirmation), not syntax-only.~~ → **OBSOLETE (diagram removed; no portal render-verify needed).**

All other #14586 ACs stand (house conventions ✓, #14548 quotes verbatim-sourced ✓, over-claim audit ✓, no client names ✓, blog.json JSON.parse-verified ✓, companion cross-link ✓, cross-family review — Euclid content-accepted + second reviewer routing in progress). Recorded per Euclid's re-review RA-1. 🖖 Grace
